questions.question: The death of Mughal Emperor Aurangzeb occurred at which among the following places?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb had died in Ahmednagar on 20 February 1707 at the age of 88. Unlike other earlier Mughals, he was buried in a modest open-air grave in Khuldabad, which shows his deep devotion to his Islamic beliefs.

questions.question: Which among the following Mughal Emperor was also known as Roshan Akhtar ?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Shah Rangile also known as Roshan Akhtar, was the Mughal emperor between 1719 and 1748. During his time, Nadir Shah attacked and looted Delhi and took the Peacock Throne with himself.

questions.question: Who was the first Indian ruler to organize Haj pilgrimage at the expense of the state?
questions.explanation: Akbar was the first Indian ruler to organize Haj pilgrimage at the expense of the state.

questions.question: Which of the following ladies wrote an historical account during the Mughal period?
questions.explanation: Gulbadan Begum (1523-1603) was a Perso-Turkic Princess, the daughter of Emperor Babur. She is most known as the author of Humayun Nama, the account of the life of her brother, Humayun.

questions.question: Which of the following Mughal rulers banned engraving Kalma on coins?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb stopped engraving Kalma on coins. Forbade the Parsis to celebrate their festival Navaroz. Released an order to ban the music everywhere and arrest those who listen to the music. His drink was plain water and he used to sleep on ground, something
